store.desktopOnly: Available in the desktop app only.

runtime.checkingAvailabilityDescription: Extensions need the configured runtime to manage plugins, MCP servers, skills, and provider connections.
runtime.failedToStartDescription: Extensions are disabled until the runtime passes its startup health check. Open the Dashboard to repair or reinstall it.
runtime.failedToStartTitle: The configured runtime was found but failed to start
runtime.multimodelCapabilitiesDescription: Provider support can differ by section. Plugins are shown only where the runtime explicitly declares support.
runtime.needsSignInDescription: {{runtime}} was found{{version}}, but plugin installs are disabled until you sign in from the Dashboard.
runtime.notAvailableDescription: Extensions are disabled until the runtime is installed. Open the Dashboard to install it and retry.
runtime.notAvailableTitle: The configured runtime is not available
runtime.readyDescription: Plugins can be installed from this page{{versionSuffix}}.
runtime.requiredForMutations: The configured runtime is required to install or uninstall extensions. Install or repair it from the Dashboard.

store.sessionsRestartWarning: Running sessions won't pick up extension changes until restarted.

apiKeys.description: Secret keys for online services. Add them here so plugins, servers, and integrations can connect and work.
apiKeys.label: API Keys

mcpServers.description: Connections to outside tools and apps. They let the runtime read data or do actions beyond this app.
mcpServers.label: MCP Servers

plugins.description: Small add-ons for the runtime. In multimodel mode they currently apply to Anthropic sessions when supported. Broader provider support is in development.
plugins.label: Plugins

skills.description: Ready-made instructions for common jobs. They help the runtime handle repeatable tasks more consistently.
skills.label: Skills

store.title: Extensions

empty.description: Check back later for new plugins
empty.filteredDescription: Try adjusting your search or filter criteria
empty.title: No plugins available

pluginsPanel.filterDescription: Narrow the catalog by category, capability, or installed state.
pluginsPanel.providerSupportNotice: Plugin support is currently guaranteed for Anthropic (Claude) sessions only. We're working to support plugins across all agents.
pluginsPanel.resultsUpdateInstantly: Results update instantly as you refine filters.

customMcp.description: Add a server manually without the catalog.

errors.invalidServerName: Invalid server name. Use alphanumeric characters, dashes, underscores, dots.

customMcp.title: Add Custom MCP Server

auth.remoteMayNeedHeaders: Remote MCP servers may still require custom headers or API keys even when the registry does not describe them. If connection fails after install, check the provider docs.

install.manualSetupDescription: This server requires manual setup. Check the repository for installation instructions.

advanced.customDescription: This skill uses a custom markdown format, so edit it directly here.
advanced.description: Most people can skip this. Open it only if you want direct control over the raw markdown file.
advanced.title: 4. Advanced SKILL.md editor

basics.description: Give this skill a clear name, choose who can use it, and decide where it should live.
basics.title: 1. Basics

description.create: Describe the workflow in plain language, review the files that will be created, then save it.
description.edit: Update this skill, review the resulting file changes, then save it.

extraFiles.assetsDescription: Add screenshots or bundled media only if they help explain the workflow.
extraFiles.description: Add supporting docs, scripts, or assets only if this skill really needs them.
extraFiles.lockedForEdits: Root and folder are locked for edits
extraFiles.optionalDescription: Add starter files that will be included in the review and written together with `SKILL.md`.
extraFiles.referencesDescription: Add supporting docs, links, or examples the runtime can look at.
extraFiles.scriptsDescription: Add helper commands or setup notes. Review carefully before sharing this skill.
extraFiles.title: 3. Extra files

fields.folderNameHint: We suggest this automatically from the skill name so review works right away.
fields.name: Skill name

instructions.description: These sections generate the skill file for you, so you do not need to edit markdown unless you want to.
instructions.locked: Structured fields are locked because you switched to manual `SKILL.md` editing below.
instructions.title: 2. Instructions

invocation.manualOnly: Only when you ask for it

placeholders.name: Write concise skill name
placeholders.notes: Example: Call out missing tests, regressions, and risky assumptions.
placeholders.steps:
1. Inspect the relevant files.
2. Explain the main risk first.
3. Suggest the safest fix.
placeholders.whenToUse: Example: Use this when the task is a code review or bug triage request.

review.hint: Review the file changes first, then confirm save in the next step.

deleteDialog.description: Delete this skill and move it to Trash?
deleteDialog.descriptionWithName: Delete "{{name}}" and move it to Trash? You can restore it later from Trash if needed.
deleteDialog.title: Delete skill?

skillDetail.descriptionFallback: Inspect discovered skill metadata and raw instructions.

invocation.auto: Runs automatically when it matches the task.
invocation.manualOnly: Only runs when you explicitly ask for it.

issues.reviewCarefully: Review this skill carefully before using it

empty.noMatchesDescription: Try a different search term or switch filters.
empty.noSkillsDescription: Create your first skill to teach a repeatable workflow, or import one you already use.

hero.codexAvailable: Use `.codex` when a skill should stay Codex-only.
hero.codexUnavailable: Existing `.codex` skills stay editable here, but new Codex-only skills need the Codex runtime enabled.
hero.description: Skills are reusable instructions that help the runtime handle the same kind of task more consistently.
hero.guidance: Use personal skills for habits you want everywhere. Use project skills for workflows that only make sense inside one codebase.
hero.personalContext: You are seeing only your personal skills right now.
hero.projectContext: You are seeing skills for {{project}} plus your personal skills.
hero.title: Teach repeatable work

invocation.manualOnly: Only runs when you explicitly ask for it

skillsPanel.runtimeAudience: Shared skills in `.claude`, `.cursor`, and `.agents` are available to {{audience}}. Skills stored in `.codex` stay Codex-only when Codex support is available.
skillsPanel.searchPlaceholder: Search by skill name or what it helps with...

personal.description: Habits and instructions you want available everywhere.
personal.title: Personal skills

project.description: Workflows that only make sense for this codebase.
project.title: Project skills

status.hasScripts: Includes scripts, so review it carefully
status.needsAttention: Needs attention before you rely on it

skillImport.title: Import skill
skillImport.description: Pick an existing skill folder, review what will be copied, then import it into one of your supported skill locations.

chooseFolder.title: 1. Choose a skill folder
chooseFolder.description: This should be a folder that already contains a `SKILL.md`, `Skill.md`, or `skill.md` file.

location.title: 2. Decide where it belongs
location.description: Personal skills work everywhere. Project skills only show up for one codebase.

skillImport.reviewHint: Review the copied files first, then confirm the import in the next step.

errors.missingSkillFile: This folder does not look like a skill yet. It needs a SKILL.md, Skill.md, or skill.md file.
errors.symbolicLinks: This folder contains symbolic links. Import the real files instead of links.
errors.tooManyFiles: This skill folder is too large to import at once. Remove extra files and try again.
errors.tooLarge: This skill folder is too large to import safely. Trim large assets and try again.
errors.invalidFolderName: Pick a simpler destination folder name using letters, numbers, dots, dashes, or underscores.
errors.mustBeDirectory: Choose a folder to import, not a single file.

health.title: MCP Health Status
health.description: Run diagnostics from this page to verify installed MCP connectivity.

disableReasons.runtimeFailedToStart: The configured runtime was found but failed to start. Open the Dashboard to repair or reinstall it.
disableReasons.runtimeRequired: The configured runtime is required. Install or repair it from the Dashboard.

runtime.requiredDescription: MCP health checks require {{runtime}}. Go to the Dashboard to install or repair it.

empty.title: No MCP servers available
empty.description: Check back later for new servers

apiKeys.description: Securely store API keys for auto-filling when installing MCP servers.

storage.osKeychain: Keys are encrypted via {{backend}} and stored with restricted file permissions (owner-only).
storage.localEncryption: OS keychain unavailable - keys are encrypted locally with AES-256. For stronger protection, install a keyring service (gnome-keyring, kwallet).

empty.title: No API keys saved
empty.description: Add keys to auto-fill environment variables when installing MCP servers.

form.addDescription: Store an API key for auto-filling in MCP server installations.
form.editDescription: Update the key details. You must re-enter the value.
form.keychainUnavailable: OS keychain unavailable - keys encrypted with AES-256 locally. Install gnome-keyring for OS-level protection.
form.name: Name

errors.invalidEnvVarFormat: Use letters, digits, underscores. Must start with a letter or underscore.
errors.projectScopeRequiresProject: Project-scoped API keys require an active project

skillReview.description: {{reviewLabel}} previews the filesystem changes first. Nothing is written until you confirm below.
skillReview.confirmPromptPrefix: Review the diff below, then use
